content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

March 27, 2017

Janet C. Kern
City Attorney
Office of the City Attorney
2263 Santa Clara Avenue, Room 280
Alameda, CA 94501-4477

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-17-006

Dear Ms. Kern:

This letter responds to your request for informal assistance on behalf of Alameda City Councilmember Maria Vella regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Because your inquiry is general in nature and does not pertain to a specific decision, we are treating it as a request for informal assistance.²

Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

FACTS

Malia Vella is a member of the Alameda City Council. She was elected in December 2016. Ms. Vella is an attorney and is an employee of the International Brotherhood of Teamsters, Local 856 ("Local 856"). You stated that Local 856 is in San Bruno, California, does not do business in the City of Alameda, has not done business in Alameda in the past two years, and does not plan to do business in Alameda.

The International Brotherhood of Teamsters ("Teamsters") is a labor union with about 1.4 million members worldwide. There are hundreds of Teamsters local unions ("Locals") across North America. The members of each Local elect their own officers, devise their own structure and vote on their own bylaws, compatible with the International's Constitution and Bylaws.

Local 856 provides representation to a diverse group of public and private sector employees. It represents civil servants in over 20 cities, counties and special districts in Northern California.

¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 84113; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

File No. I-17-006
Page No. 2

including law enforcement (police officers, probation officers, deputy district attorneys, non-sworn
personnel), firefighters, maintenance workers, clerical office professionals, librarians, engineers,
building and code enforcement and water and wastewater technicians. It also represents hotel
workers including front desk personnel and housekeepers, workers in the airline industry such as
aircraft mechanics and customer service representatives, healthcare workers, benefit administrators,
rental car personnel, and many other professions such as those employed in the package, warehouse
and distribution industry, animal services, freight, vending services and the tradeshow industry.

Employees represented by Local 856 work for: nearly every large hotel in San Francisco
and the Bay Area Peninsula; airline carriers such as United, US Airways/American and Air Canada
at all major airports in the Bay Area and Sacramento; hospitals and medical facilities all over the
Bay Area including San Francisco General, St. Rose, Marin General, Eden Medical Center and
Washington Hospital; and rental car companies such as Alamo, Avis, Budget, Hertz and National.

Employees of the City of Alameda are organized and are represented by various
Associations and unions, including the International Association of Firefighters, Local 689, the
Operating Engineers, Local 3 and the International Brotherhood of Electrical Workers, Local 1245.
Local 856 does not represent any employee organization of the City of Alameda. City management
and representatives of these Associations/unions engage in collective bargaining concerning
wages, hours and working conditions. These issues are typically discussed in closed session with
the City Council under Government Code, Section 54957.6. When agreements are reached with
the various bargaining groups, the City Council in open session takes action to approve such
agreements.

Ms. Vella filed campaign statements in connection with her campaign to be elected to City
Council. Those statements indicate that several union locals contributed to her campaign, including
Operating Engineers Local 3, the Sprinklers Fitters and Apprentices, Local 483 and the Sheet Metal
Workers, Local 104. In addition, the California Teamsters Public Affairs Council Public Affairs
Fund also contributed to her campaign. As also required by law, Ms. Vella has filed her "Assuming
Office" statement. She did not list Local 856 as a source of income because Local 856, as stated
above, is not located in Alameda, is not doing business in Alameda, is not planning on doing
business in Alameda and has not done business in Alameda in the past two years.

You wish to know whether under the Act, Councilmember Vella may participate in
governmental decisions related to: (1) members represented by Local 856; (2) employers of
employees that Local 856 represents; (3) employees of the City who are represented by current
bargaining groups, organizations, and individuals who contributed to Ms. Vella's election campaign
for City Council; and (4) applications generally where unions (other than Local 856) may be
involved financially.

DISCUSSION

Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in making, or using
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a financial
interest.

File No. I-17-006
Page No. 3

Section 87103 provides that a public official has a "financial interest" in a governmental
decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material financial effect on one
or more of the public official's interests. Section 87103 also sets forth the interests from which a
conflict of interest may arise under the Act. They include:

• Any source of income, except gifts or loans by a commercial lending institution made in the
regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).)

As an employee of Local 856, the labor union is a source of income¹ to Councilmember
Vella if she received or was promised income aggregating $500 or more in value within 12 months
of the time a decision is made.

It is unclear at this time what type of specific decisions Councilmember Vella will be
making, participating in, or influencing as a public official that involves the following groups:

• Members represented by Local 856;
• Employers of employees that Local 856 represents;
• Employees of the City who are represented by bargaining groups, organizations, or
individuals who contributed⁴ to Ms. Vella's election campaign for City Council;
• Or applications where unions (other than Local 856) may be involved financially.

Therefore, we are only able to provide the following general discussion of the conflict of
interest rules.

Foreseeability:

Under the Act, an effect on an interest is presumed foreseeable if the interest is explicitly
involved in the decision, and an interest is "explicitly involved" if the interest is a named party in,
or subject of, the decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) In this case, Councilmember Vella's interest in
Local 856 will be explicitly involved in a decision if it is a named party in, or the subject of, a
governmental decision before the Alameda City Council.⁵

¹ Under the facts presented, the only source of income to Councilmember Vella is her employer, Local 856, not
affiliated entities. Generally, we will not "pierce" through an association that is a source of income to an official, to
attribute the income to the individual members of the association, where the members do not control the employment
relationship between the official and the association. (Abt Advice Letter, No. A-91-361; Navi Advice Letter,
No. A-84-013.)
² With regard to campaign contributions, Section 84308 prohibits officers of certain agencies from accepting,
soliciting, or directing a contribution of $250 or more from parties or participants in a proceeding involving a license,
permit, or other entitlement for use pending before the agency during a specified time period. However,
Section 84308 does not apply to local government agencies whose members are directly elected by the voters.
(Section 84308(b).) Because Councilmember Vella is a local elected official, the prohibitions in Section 84308 are
not applicable to her. (See Cluster Advice Letter No. A-86-017.
³ A public official's financial interests are the "subject of" a proceeding if the decision involves the issuance,
renewal, approval, denial or revocation of any license, permit, or other entitlement to, or contract with, the financial
interest, and includes any governmental decision affecting a real property interest.

File No. I-17-006
Page No. 4

When a public official's interests are not explicitly involved in the decision, an effect of the decision on one or more of the official's interests is only reasonably foreseeable if it can be recognized as a realistic possibility and more than hypothetical or theoretical. (Regulation 18701(b).) Regulation 18701(b) suggests the following nonexclusive list of factors should be considered.

(1) The extent to which the occurrence of the financial effect is contingent upon intervening events not including future governmental decisions by the official's agency, or any other agency appointed by or subject to the budgetary control of the official's agency.

(2) Whether the public official should anticipate a financial effect on his or her financial interest as a potential outcome under normal circumstances when using appropriate due diligence and care.

(3) Whether the public official has a financial interest that is of the type that would typically be affected by the terms of the governmental decision or whether the governmental decision is of the type that would be expected to have a financial effect on businesses and individuals similarly situated to those businesses and individuals in which the public official has a financial interest.

(4) Whether a reasonable inference can be made that the financial effects of the governmental decision on the public official's financial interest might compromise a public official's ability to act in a manner consistent with his or her duty to act in the best interests of the public.

(5) Whether the governmental decision will provide or deny an opportunity, or create an advantage or disadvantage for one of the official's financial interests, including whether the financial interest may be entitled to compete or be eligible for a benefit resulting from the decision.

(6) Whether the public official has the type of financial interest that would cause a similarly situated person to weigh the advantages and disadvantages of the governmental decision on his or her financial interest in formulating a position.

Materiality:

The following standard in Regulation 18702.3 is used to determine whether the reasonably foreseeable financial effect of a governmental decision on Ms. Vella's financial interest in Local 856, a source of income, is material:

(a) . . . For income received by the official . . . including salary, the financial effect is material if:

(1) The source is a claimant, applicant, respondent, contracting party, or is otherwise named or identified as the subject of the proceeding; or

* * *

File No. I-17-006
Page No. 5

(3) The source is a nonprofit that will receive a measurable financial benefit or loss,
or the official knows or has reason to know that the nonprofit has an interest in real
property that will be financially affected under the standards applied to a financial
interest in Regulation 18702.2.

Therefore, Councilmember Vella may not participate in decisions before the city council
involving the various aforementioned parties if Local 856 is also the subject of the decision, the
decision will result in a measurable financial benefit or loss to Local 856, or it will result in a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on real property in which Local 856 has an interest.

Disqualification:

When a public official who holds an office specified in Section 87200 (such as a city
council member) has a conflict of interest in a decision noticed at a public meeting, he or she must:
(1) immediately prior to the discussion of the item, orally identify each type of economic interest
involved in the decision as well as details of the economic interest on the record of the meeting; (2)
recuse himself or herself, and (3) leave the room for the duration of the discussion and/or vote on
the item. (Section 87105; Regulation 18707.)

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: Emelyn C. Rodriguez
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

ECR:jgl

regular course of business on terms available to the public without regard to official status, 
aggregating $500 or more in value provided or promised to, received by, the public official 
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made. (Section 87103(c).) 
As an employee of Local 856, the labor union is a source of income3 to Councilmember 
Vella if she received or was promised income aggregating $500 or more in value within 12 months

• Employees of the City who are represented by bargaining groups, organizations, or 
individuals who contributed4 to Ms. Vella's election campaign for City Council; 
• Or applications where unions (other than Local 856) may be involved financially.

or subject of, the decision. (Regulation 18701(a).) In this case, Councilmember Vella's interest in

interest as a potential outcome under normal circumstances when using appropriate due diligence

sections.question_synthetic: May Alameda City Councilmember Maria Vella participate in governmental decisions involving Local 856 (her employer), its represented members and employers, City employees represented by unions that contributed to her campaign, and applications involving other unions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC provides general guidance on conflict of interest analysis under the Political Reform Act, explaining that Vella's participation depends on whether Local 856's interests are explicitly involved in specific decisions and whether any financial effects would be reasonably foreseeable and material, rather than providing a definitive yes/no answer without knowing the specific decisions at issue.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, old; LLM: Document appears incomplete (truncated); formal conclusion section not provided in available text. Marked as informal advice letter per opening statement; no immunity protection applies. Mixed regulatory/statutory citations throughout. Comprehensive factual background provided regarding Local 856's operations and Vella's employment relationship.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You wish to know whether under the Act, Councilmember Vella may participate in governmental decisions related to: (1) members represented by Local 856; (2) employers of employees that Local 856 represents; (3) employees of the City who are represented by current bargaining groups, organizations, and individuals who contributed to Ms. Vella's election campaign for City Council; and (4) applications generally where unions (other than Local 856) may be involved financially.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC provides general guidance on conflict of interest analysis under the Political Reform Act, explaining that Vella's participation depends on whether Local 856's interests are explicitly involved in specific decisions and whether any financial effects would be reasonably foreseeable and material, rather than providing a definitive yes/no answer without knowing the specific decisions at issue.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter responding to City Attorney Janet Kern's request regarding Councilmember Maria Vella's ability to participate in governmental decisions where she may have conflicting interests as an employee of International Brotherhood of Teamsters Local 856. The FPPC provides general analysis of conflict of interest provisions under the Political Reform Act, explaining foreseeability and materiality standards without making specific conclusions pending knowledge of particular decisions.
